mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during easing periods do not exceed baseline by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices reprice downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month window signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth forecasts downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 757085.0M and HOUST at 1502.0K as of 2026-03-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or mean 60-day returns during joint contraction periods exceed baseline by > 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, it signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth < 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 200,000 persons per month over a 3-month rolling average signals robust labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during periods of strong payroll growth do not exceed baseline by at least 100 basis points (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the central bank is in a restrictive monetary stance that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.64% and CPIAUCSL at 332.407 as of 2026-04-01
regime_hint: restrictive monetary tightening with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or mean 60-day returns during joint high-rate/high-inflation periods exceed baseline by > 150 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1.6 million units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, it signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence that supports equity valuations through higher expected earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.6M annualized AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 1.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1502.0K as of 2026-03-01, GDPC1 at 24174.527 as of 2026-01-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or mean 5-day returns during joint housing strength/GDP growth periods do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points.
